sequence_drift.detail: All technical noun phrases resolve to 2.2's concepts_introduced (while loop, for loop and loop variable, range() start/stop/step, enumerate() and zip(), break and continue, nested loops, loop else clause) or allowed prior material (1.2-1.4 variables/types, comparison + boolean operators, print/input/int conversion, modulo; 2.1 conditionals: if inside loop, indentation-as-block). No use of def/functions, list/tuple/dict/set literals, comprehensions, or .append. enumerate()/zip() multi-value results are described as 'two things'/'pairs' and destructured via 'unpacks into two loop variables' without introducing tuple terminology, staying in-scope.

'unpacks the two values' (line 131) is described operationally, not as tuple unpacking. Soft observations only, not fails.
